seo.page_title: 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G import to NZ
seo.meta_description: Import a 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G to NZ with 62 kWh battery, up to 385 km range and SOH checked. Cut running costs and charge at home.

seo.og_title: 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G | Import to NZ
seo.og_description: Looking at a 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G? We help you import from Japan with SOH checks, clear landed cost and support through compliance.
seo.primary_keyword: 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G import NZ
secondary_keywords: Leaf E+ G Japan to NZ

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Cut your running costs with a 62 kWh 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G, set up for NZ driving and charging, with battery state of health (SOH) checked before you commit.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: The 2021 Leaf E+ G gives you a 62 kWh battery, WLTP range up to 385 km and 0–100 km/h in about 7 seconds, so it handles both the school run and motorway trips. Most owners charge at home overnight and use public CHAdeMO fast chargers for longer drives. It’s a practical way to slash fuel bills while keeping daily driving simple.

savings_block.headline: What you could save vs buying a Leaf E+ G on the yard
savings_block.body: Recent 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ Gs at NZ dealers often sit above what similar cars sell for at Japanese auctions. We typically see landed costs in the $32,000–$42,000 range, depending on mileage, grade and options, once shipping, NZ entry certification and on-road costs are added. Importing lets you target the condition and SOH you want, rather than taking whatever is on the lot.

cta_form.headline: Find your Leaf E+ G — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Here’s a 2021 Leaf E+ G walkaround, showing the 17-inch alloys, hatchback boot space and the simple EV driving controls you’ll use every day.
blocks.main_usp_deep: With its 62 kWh lithium-ion battery and WLTP range up to 385 km, the 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G works well for NZ-wide commuting without constant top-ups. The 160 kW motor gives brisk acceleration for merging and overtaking, while front-wheel drive keeps things predictable in wet conditions. ProPILOT, AEB and lane keep assist add a layer of safety for busy motorways and city traffic. Inside, Apple CarPlay/Android Auto, heated seats and 5-seat hatchback practicality make it easy to live with day to day.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You’re a commuter or small family who wants to charge at home, cut fuel spend and still have enough range for weekend trips. You’re more interested in low running costs and verified battery health than badges. For that mix of needs, a 2021 Leaf E+ G is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’re an NZ-based team that’s been importing cars from Japan since 2009. Over that time we’ve helped bring in thousands of vehicles, including many Nissan Leafs, for buyers across the country. You get full auction documentation, photos and clear explanations before you decide to bid. Our role is to make the process transparent, from auction price through to compliance and delivery.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives enough capacity for most NZ commutes and regular weekend trips without needing to charge every day.
On typical NZ roads you can comfortably cover daily driving and still have a buffer for errands or detours.
Delivers quick response for motorway on-ramps and passing lanes, while staying easy to handle in the wet.
Lets you top up quickly on longer trips using NZ’s growing CHAdeMO network at supermarkets, malls and highway stops.

car_specs_benefits.spec: Apple CarPlay/Android Auto, heated seats, 5-seat hatchback
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Keeps the family comfortable and connected, with enough space for school bags, groceries and weekend gear.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how the 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G typically lines up when you’re comparing options to import.

rows.notes: The E+ G with the 62 kWh battery is available from around 2019 onwards, with 2021 cars benefiting from newer battery age and updated safety tech. Later years can help with future resale and may show less battery wear, but they usually cost more at auction.

As a full EV, the Leaf E+ G uses a 62 kWh lithium-ion battery and 160 kW motor only, with no petrol backup. Earlier or lower trims often have a 40 kWh pack, which is cheaper but offers less range, so think about your daily distance and charging options.

The E+ G trim typically adds the larger 62 kWh battery, 160 kW motor, 17-inch alloys and more safety and comfort features compared with base models. Some cars will also have extras like ProPILOT, heated seats and upgraded audio, which we can confirm from the auction sheet.

2021 Leafs typically meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. As an EV there’s no Clean Car fee, and you may qualify for lower running costs, but normal entry certification checks still apply.

Leaf E+ G models appear regularly at major Japanese auctions, but low-kilometre, high-SOH examples attract strong bidding. If you’re flexible on colour, mileage or options, we can usually find a suitable car more quickly.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For EVs like the Leaf E+ G, we focus on battery SOH, usage notes and any comments about charging on the auction sheet before shortlisting.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common specs or colours, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a Leaf E+ G is straightforward, but it helps to understand the main risks and how we handle them.

items.risk: You’re worried about battery degradation, SOH and the cost of a future battery replacement.
items.solution: We focus on SOH readings, age and mileage, and avoid cars with obvious warning signs in the documentation. We explain what the SOH figure likely means for real-world range so you can decide if it suits your plans.

items.risk: Hidden costs between the auction price and getting the car on your driveway.
items.solution: We break down auction price, shipping, insurance, NZ entry certification, port charges and on-road costs so you can see the likely landed cost before you commit to bidding.

items.risk: Uncertainty about auction grades and what they really mean for condition.
items.solution: We explain the auction grade, translate key comments and highlight any notes about repairs, paintwork or interior wear. You get photos and context, not just a single grade number.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: Tell us the year, budget and range you’re aiming for, and we’ll shortlist suitable 2021 Leaf E+ Gs from the Japanese auctions. You review the options, ask questions and only move ahead when you’re comfortable with the SOH and total cost. If you’d like to explore it further, send through a brief and we’ll come back with real examples.

faq_block.q: What real-world range can I expect from a 2021 Leaf E+ G in NZ?
faq_block.a: The WLTP figure is up to 385 km, but in NZ conditions most drivers see less, especially at motorway speeds or in winter. We use SOH, your driving pattern and local terrain to give a realistic range estimate before you buy.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2021 Nissan Leaf E+ G?
faq_block.a: Most buyers land these between about $32,000 and $42,000, depending on mileage, SOH, grade and options. The total includes the auction price, our fee, shipping, insurance, NZ entry certification, port charges and on-road costs.

faq_block.q: Will a 2021 Leaf E+ G usually meet NZ compliance standards?
faq_block.a: Based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. Final approval depends on physical inspection at entry certification, which we can’t guarantee in advance.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving the car in NZ?
faq_block.a: From a successful bid, allow roughly 6–10 weeks for shipping, port processing, NZ entry certification and registration. Timing varies with shipping schedules, port congestion and how quickly compliance inspections can be completed.

faq_block.q: How do auction grades relate to the condition of a Leaf E+ G?
faq_block.a: Auction grades give a snapshot of exterior, interior and overall condition, but the comments and photos matter just as much. We translate key notes, highlight any repairs or damage and give you our view so you’re not buying blind.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based importer working directly with Japanese auctions since 2009. We focus on matching buyers with specific models like the Nissan Leaf, using full auction documentation and clear pricing. Our aim is to make importing a practical alternative to buying off the yard.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost is the total to get a 2021 Leaf E+ G from the Japanese auction to registered on NZ roads.

rows.notes: The hammer price in Japan, plus local auction and inland transport fees where applicable.
Roll-on roll-off shipping from Japan to NZ and marine insurance to cover the car in transit.
Entry certification inspection, structural and safety checks, registration, plates and related port and processing charges.

auction_checklist.check: Battery SOH reading and age of the car
auction_checklist.why: Higher SOH and a younger 2021 pack usually mean better range now and slower degradation over time.
Gives a clearer picture of how the Leaf has been treated and whether there are repairs or heavy wear to factor in.
Mileage combined with SOH helps indicate whether the car has done lots of rapid charging or mainly gentle commuting.

auction_checklist.check: Charging equipment and CHAdeMO port condition
auction_checklist.why: You’ll want the correct cables and a clean, undamaged fast-charge port for easy use in NZ.

auction_checklist.check: Safety features like ProPILOT, AEB and lane keep assist
auction_checklist.why: These systems can make daily driving and long trips safer and less tiring on NZ roads.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You regularly drive very long distances in areas with limited CHAdeMO coverage and don’t want to plan charging stops.
You prefer a plug-in hybrid or petrol backup rather than relying fully on public and home charging.
You’re looking for a very low purchase price and are willing to accept an older, smaller-battery EV instead.

alternatives_block.intro: If the 62 kWh Leaf E+ G doesn’t quite match your range, budget or charging plans, there are other models worth a look.

rows.better_if: You mainly do short city trips and want a lower upfront cost.
rows.tradeoff: Less range than the 62 kWh E+ G, so more frequent charging and less flexibility for longer drives.

battery_health_block.headline: Battery health on the 2021 Leaf E+ G
battery_health_block.body: The 62 kWh pack in a 2021 Leaf E+ G is relatively new, but SOH still varies depending on how the car’s been used and charged. We treat SOH as a key buying factor, not an afterthought. By combining SOH readings, mileage and auction comments, we help you avoid cars that may have had heavy fast-charging or hard use.
checklist: Confirm SOH reading from clear dash photos on the auction listing
Compare SOH against odometer to spot unusual wear patterns
Check for any battery or EV system warnings in the auction notes
Favour cars with consistent service history where available
Discuss what the SOH figure likely means for your daily range

charging_block.headline: Charging a 2021 Leaf E+ G in NZ
charging_block.home_charging: Most owners charge at home using a dedicated wall unit or a standard socket, topping up the 62 kWh battery overnight. This lets you leave each morning with a full charge and keeps running costs low, especially if you use off-peak power rates.
charging_block.public_charging: On the road, the Leaf E+ G uses CHAdeMO fast charging, which is available across much of NZ at supermarkets, shopping centres and highway stops. You can also use slower AC chargers in many public carparks while you shop or work.
caveats: CHAdeMO is well established in NZ but newer networks may focus more on CCS, so check coverage on your regular routes.
Fast charging too often can add wear to the battery over time, so it’s best kept for trips rather than daily use.
Charging speeds can slow down as the battery fills or in hot conditions, so allow some buffer time on long journeys.
